home.title: Create Games with AI — No Code Required
home.description: Turn your game ideas into playable browser games in minutes. Describe what you want, AI builds it, and publish it for the world.
home.heroSignalIdeaDetail: Your first game builds in under a minute
home.heroSignalCrewDetail: Multiple AI agents working on your game at once
home.heroSignalLaunchDetail: Tokens and publishing baked right in
home.heroTitle: Turn a sentence into a playable game in under a minute.
home.heroDescription: Atomic is an AI-powered game engine where you describe what you want and a crew of specialized agents builds it live — 2D or 3D, complete with assets, logic, and a shareable link.
home.sectionHowItWorksTitle: Three moves from idea to shareable game.
home.sectionHowItWorksDescription: No installs, no boilerplate. Just describe, watch, and share.
home.howStep1Description: Type a sentence or a paragraph. Pick 2D or 3D. Choose a genre template or go freeform.
home.howStep2Description: A crew of specialized agents — orchestrator, builder, designer, QA — work in parallel in the War Room.
home.howStep3Description: Your game is playable instantly. Publish it with a link, iterate with feature threads, or launch a token.
home.sectionAgentCrewTitle: Meet the agents behind every game.
home.sectionAgentCrewDescription: Each agent is a specialist. Together they turn your prompt into a playable game.
home.agentJarvisDescription: Breaks your prompt into a task graph, assigns work, and makes sure the whole pipeline finishes clean.
home.agentForgeDescription: Writes the game code — physics, controls, scene setup — atom by atom inside the Phaser or Three.js runtime.
home.agentPixelDescription: Generates and integrates sprites, models, animations, and UI layouts that match your game's art direction.
home.agentCheckerDescription: Validates every build, catches runtime errors, and confirms the game matches what you asked for.
home.sectionFeaturesTitle: Everything you need to go from idea to launch.
home.sectionFeaturesDescription: A full creation stack — not just a chatbot with a code editor.
home.featureWarRoomDescription: Watch your game being built in real-time with full pipeline visibility and step-by-step progress.
home.featureThreadsDescription: Iterate on specific mechanics, visuals, or logic in focused chat threads that update atoms directly.
home.featureLibraryDescription: Browse, filter, and manage all your creations — published or in progress — in one visual dashboard.
home.featureTokensDescription: Attach a token to your game with one click. Configure supply, allocations, and leaderboard rewards.
home.featureOpenClawDescription: Connect external AI agents to your Atomic workspace via a fully scoped API with webhooks and heartbeats.
home.featureAnalyticsDescription: Track plays, engagement, and growth across your published games with built-in creator analytics.
home.sectionSocialDescription: From jam entries to full releases, the community is shipping games every day.
home.testimonial1: I shipped a tower defense game for a game jam in 40 minutes. The AI agents handled the pathfinding and wave system while I focused on the theme.
home.testimonial2: We use Atomic to prototype game ideas before committing to full production. It's replaced our internal grey-box workflow entirely.
home.testimonial3: My students build playable games in a single class period. It's the fastest path from concept to something you can actually share.
home.sectionPricingDescription: No credit card needed. Upgrade only when your games need more power.

dashboard.noGames: You haven't created any games yet.
dashboard.heroTitle: Turn Your Wildest Idea Into a Playable Game in Seconds
dashboard.heroDescription: Chat → Pick Genre → Instant Game + Token. No coding required, just your imagination.
dashboard.noCreationsYet: No creations yet. Start a game from the dashboard to see it here.

games.title: My Games
games.gameLiveDescription: Your game is live! Share the link or unpublish it.
games.makePlayableDescription: Make your game playable by anyone with the link.
games.updateTokenDescription: Update your game token settings. Blockchain deployment coming soon.
games.configureTokenDescription: Configure a token for your game. This saves a draft — blockchain deployment is coming soon.
games.leaderboardAllocationDescription: Top leaderboard players will receive {pct}% of the total supply when the token launches.

wizard.launchTitle: Launch a new game in four sharp moves
wizard.launchDescription: Shape the concept, choose 2D or 3D, pick a starter scaffold, and ship directly into the editor.
wizard.nameYourGameDescription: Give your game a unique name. You can add a description later.
wizard.setToneHelper: Set the tone now, refine the details later.
wizard.chooseGameFormatDescription: Set the project direction first. The next step only shows starter genres that fit the format you pick.
wizard.game2dDescription: Sprite-led movement, side views, top-down spaces, and tighter arcade framing.
wizard.game3dDescription: Full-scene depth, spatial cameras, and worlds built around movement through space.
wizard.chooseGenreDescription: Pick a starting template for your {format}. Each genre comes with pre-built atoms and a scaffold matched to that direction.
wizard.reviewDescription: One final pass before the wizard generates your starter game and opens the editor.
wizard.cardUpdatesWithChoice: The card updates with each choice.
wizard.reviewFallbackCopy: A polished new playground for AI-assisted mechanics, bold visuals, and instant iteration.
wizard.hintStep0: Press Enter to continue. Use Cmd/Ctrl+Enter in the description box.
wizard.hintStep1: Choose 2D or 3D, then press Enter to continue.
wizard.hintStep2: Choose a template, then press Enter to continue.
wizard.hintStep3: Press Enter to create once everything looks right.
wizard.errorNameRequired: Give your game a name before continuing.
wizard.errorNameTooLong: Game names must be {length} characters or less.
wizard.errorNameBeforeGenre: Give your game a name before choosing a genre.
wizard.errorNameBeforeReview: Give your game a name before reviewing it.
wizard.errorNameBeforeCreate: Give your game a name before creating it.
wizard.errorSignIn: Sign in to create a game.
wizard.errorDuplicateName: That game name already exists. Try a different one.

workspace.chooseWorkstreamDescription: Open a feature thread, revisit a war room run, or start a new chat from the workstream rail.

chat.newFeatureThreadDescription: Describe a focused feature, fix, or gameplay improvement and the builder chat will work directly in atoms.

settings.title: Settings
settings.manageProfileTitle: Manage your profile and keep the platform's core runtime details in one place.
settings.manageProfileDescription: This page keeps account edits, system health, supported models, and operating docs attached to the same creator surface.
settings.changesPersistNote: Changes persist to your authenticated `user_profiles` row and are isolated to your account.
settings.unableToSaveProfile: Unable to save your profile right now.
settings.creatorProfileDescription: Update the public-facing identity attached to your account. Only profile fields are editable here in v1.
settings.platformOverviewDescription: Read-only runtime facts surfaced from the app configuration and current health contract.
settings.docsAndRunbooksDescription: Quick links to the repo-hosted references that explain how this platform is wired and deployed.
settings.connectedIdentitiesDescription: These values are read-only here and reflect the active authenticated session.
settings.systemHealthDescription: Safe service checks from the internal health route. No secrets are exposed here.
settings.accountActionsDescription: Use sign out to end the active creator session from this device.
settings.devBypassSignOutDisabled: Local development auth bypass is active, so sign-out is disabled for this session.
settings.signOutDescription: Signing out returns you to the login flow and clears the active authenticated session.
settings.unableToRefreshHealth: Unable to refresh health right now.

library.title: Game Library
library.description: Browse, filter, and manage your creations in one place.
library.noGamesMatchFilter: No games match the current filters.

openclaw.title: OpenClaw
openclaw.loadingDocsDescription: Preparing the import contract, claim flow, and operator examples.
openclaw.docsHeading: Import flow, runtime contract, and operator surface for Atomic Coding.
openclaw.docsDescription: Use these docs inside OpenClaw settings when attaching Atomic as an external control plane. The import remains owner scoped, Mastra remains built in, and the returned API key carries full owner parity across the platform.
openclaw.importStep1: Open `/openclaw` inside Atomic Coding and generate a claim session.
openclaw.importStep2: Paste the one-time claim URL into OpenClaw settings so it can read the handshake instructions.
openclaw.importStep3: POST the identity payload back to the same claim URL using the required `name` and `agent_url` fields.
openclaw.importStep4: Store the returned `api_key`, `api_base_url`, `skill_json_url`, and `heartbeat_url` inside OpenClaw.
openclaw.importStep5: Call the heartbeat endpoint roughly every five minutes and optionally configure webhooks for build and war room events.

warroom.title: War Room Console
warroom.description: Inline operator view for the live pipeline
warroom.pipelineStepsDescription: Task-level diagnostics and dependency state across the full run
warroom.agentStatesDescription: Open each agent lane for diagnostics, retries, validation notes, and recent events
warroom.noAgentsYet: No agents dispatched yet — waiting for pipeline to start.
warroom.pipelineFinished: The pipeline finished — your canvas is loading.
warroom.checkWarRoom: Check the War Room console for details.
warroom.spinNewFeature: Spin a new feature thread from this run

platformAid.speechBubble: Hey pal! Drop me a message!
platformAid.agentDescription: Route-aware help for onboarding, navigation, publishing, OpenClaw, and platform workflows.
platformAid.emptyState: Ask about where to start, how publishing works, what OpenClaw does, how War Room fits in, or where a feature lives.
platformAid.placeholder: Ask about the platform, onboarding, publishing, OpenClaw, or where something lives.
platformAid.temporarilyUnavailable: Atomic Aid Agent is temporarily unavailable. Try again shortly.
